steps.title: Compare the two findings
steps.content: Text 1 says Cohen and Nyberg found loss aversion in financial decisions generally. Text 2 narrows that idea by concluding that loss aversion appeared only in high-stakes contexts, not in low-stakes ones.

steps.title: State the likely response
steps.content: Choice A focuses on data collection rather than stakes. Choice B is too broad because Barak and Levin would add a limitation. Choice C misstates the issue as an explanation for other fields. They would say the effect depends on outcome size. The answer is D.
